Despite efforts in renewable energy, global CO₂ emissions reached a record high of 37.4 gigatons in 2023, as reported by the Global Carbon Project led by Pierre Friedlingstein.

The European Union has made progress with a 32% emissions cut since 1990 under the European Green Deal, while Brazil faces criticism for Amazon deforestation and India's net zero target by 2070 is deemed too late by climate advocates.
Major oil companies spent $750 million on lobbying against climate legislation between 2020 and 2024, as revealed by InfluenceMap, with Shell rejecting the findings.
Ten years after the Paris Agreement, global efforts to limit climate change to 1.5°C above pre-industrial levels are falling short, according to recent reports.
The Intergovernmental Panel on Climate Change (IPCC) reported a global average temperature increase of 1.45°C in 2024, dangerously close to the 1.5°C threshold.
The Global Carbon Project highlighted that despite the growth of renewable energy, fossil fuel use remains high, with South and Southeast Asia's coal consumption offsetting gains elsewhere.

The European Union has made measurable progress with a 32% reduction in emissions since 1990, but Brazil under President Lula da Silva faces criticism for Amazon deforestation, reaching 11,568 km² in 2024.
India's promise to become carbon-neutral by 2070 is criticized as too late by climate activists, while the Australian Climate Council calls it a betrayal to island nations.

A report by InfluenceMap revealed that oil and gas companies spent $750 million on lobbying against climate legislation between 2020 and 2024, with Shell's Wael Sawan dismissing the findings as selective and misleading.

UN Secretary-General António Guterres called for enhanced Nationally Determined Contributions (NDCs) by 2035 at COP30 in Belém, Brazil, highlighting that while the tools exist, political will is lacking.

The renewable energy sector offers a glimmer of hope with solar capacity surpassing 2 terawatts globally in 2024, and Denmark's Ørsted and Spain's Iberdrola reporting record investment years. Battery storage costs have also decreased by 89% since 2010.
